Phylogenetics in R

env dataset

Louis du Plessis

Last modified: 23 Jun 2026

env dataset

First we read in the alignment.

env <- read.dna(params$envfile, format = "fasta")

We can check to see that env contains 132 sequences of length 820. We can also display the names of the sequences contained in each.

We also extract the names of the sequences that stem from the patient, the victim, and the community.

env
## 132 DNA sequences in binary format stored in a matrix.
## 
## All sequences of same length: 820 
## 
## Labels:
## AY156764.1|LA26.EN
## AY156767.1|LA29.EN
## AY156769.1|LA31.EN
## AY156746.1|LA04.EN
## AY156765.1|LA27.EN
## AY156759.1|LA18.EN
## ...
## 
## Base composition:
##     a     c     g     t 
## 0.425 0.156 0.203 0.215 
## (Total: 108.24 kb)
labels(env)
##   [1] "AY156764.1|LA26.EN"   "AY156767.1|LA29.EN"   "AY156769.1|LA31.EN"  
##   [4] "AY156746.1|LA04.EN"   "AY156765.1|LA27.EN"   "AY156759.1|LA18.EN"  
##   [7] "AY156757.1|LA16.EN"   "AY156768.1|LA30.EN"   "AY156755.1|LA14.EN"  
##  [10] "AY156743.1|P.ENV"     "AY156745.1|LA02.EN"   "AY156747.1|LA05.EN"  
##  [13] "AY156752.1|LA10.EN"   "AY156761.1|LA22.EN"   "AY156888.1|V31"      
##  [16] "AY156881.1|V24"       "AY156871.1|V14"       "AY156873.1|V16"      
##  [19] "AY156903.1|V46"       "AY156744.1|V.ENV"     "AY156890.1|V33"      
##  [22] "AY156895.1|V38"       "AY156902.1|V45"       "AY156899.1|V42"      
##  [25] "AY156876.1|V19"       "AY156901.1|V44"       "AY156896.1|V39"      
##  [28] "AY156907.1|V50"       "AY156882.1|V25"       "AY156885.1|V28"      
##  [31] "AY156804.1|V1.MIC.EN" "AY156883.1|V26"       "AY156878.1|V21"      
##  [34] "AY156867.1|V10"       "AY156891.1|V34"       "AY156805.1|V2.MIC.EN"
##  [37] "AY156862.1|V05"       "AY156864.1|V07"       "AY156868.1|V11"      
##  [40] "AY156866.1|V09"       "AY156869.1|V12"       "AY156865.1|V08"      
##  [43] "AY156889.1|V32"       "AY156880.1|V23"       "AY156887.1|V30"      
##  [46] "AY156898.1|V41"       "AY156874.1|V17"       "AY156892.1|V35"      
##  [49] "AY156894.1|V37"       "AY156904.1|V47"       "AY156861.1|V04"      
##  [52] "AY156906.1|V49"       "AY156859.1|V02"       "AY156860.1|V03"      
##  [55] "AY156863.1|V06"       "AY156872.1|V15"       "AY156897.1|V40"      
##  [58] "AY156886.1|V29"       "AY156870.1|V13"       "AY156893.1|V36"      
##  [61] "AY156875.1|V18"       "AY156858.1|V01"       "AY156900.1|V43"      
##  [64] "AY156877.1|V20"       "AY156884.1|V27"       "AY156905.1|V48"      
##  [67] "AY156879.1|V22"       "AY156843.1|P36"       "AY156830.1|P23"      
##  [70] "AY156855.1|P48"       "AY156820.1|P13"       "AY156849.1|P42"      
##  [73] "AY156854.1|P47"       "AY156857.1|P50"       "AY156821.1|P14"      
##  [76] "AY156835.1|P28"       "AY156836.1|P29"       "AY156837.1|P30"      
##  [79] "AY156856.1|P49"       "AY156842.1|P35"       "AY156796.1|P1.MIC.EN"
##  [82] "AY156798.1|P2.MIC.EN" "AY156812.1|P05"       "AY156815.1|P08"      
##  [85] "AY156817.1|P10"       "AY156818.1|P11"       "AY156822.1|P15"      
##  [88] "AY156827.1|P20"       "AY156845.1|P38"       "AY156811.1|P04"      
##  [91] "AY156823.1|P16"       "AY156829.1|P22"       "AY156839.1|P32"      
##  [94] "AY156848.1|P41"       "AY156847.1|P40"       "AY156810.1|P03"      
##  [97] "AY156816.1|P09"       "AY156850.1|P43"       "AY156819.1|P12"      
## [100] "AY156832.1|P25"       "AY156844.1|P37"       "AY156833.1|P26"      
## [103] "AY156852.1|P45"       "AY156825.1|P18"       "AY156809.1|P02"      
## [106] "AY156808.1|P01"       "AY156814.1|P07"       "AY156831.1|P24"      
## [109] "AY156834.1|P27"       "AY156840.1|P33"       "AY156841.1|P34"      
## [112] "AY156853.1|P46"       "AY156813.1|P06"       "AY156824.1|P17"      
## [115] "AY156826.1|P19"       "AY156838.1|P31"       "AY156851.1|P44"      
## [118] "AY156828.1|P21"       "AY156846.1|P39"       "AY156750.1|LA08.EN"  
## [121] "AY156770.1|LA32.EN"   "AY156758.1|LA17.EN"   "AY156762.1|LA24.EN"  
## [124] "AY156763.1|LA25.EN"   "AY156754.1|LA13.EN"   "AY156748.1|LA06.EN"  
## [127] "AY156753.1|LA12.EN"   "AY156751.1|LA09.EN"   "AY156766.1|LA28.EN"  
## [130] "AY156760.1|LA20.EN"   "AY156749.1|LA07.EN"   "AY156756.1|LA15.EN"
allpatients_env <- sapply(strsplit(labels(env), "\\|"), function(x) x[2])
patient_env     <- labels(env)[grep("^P", allpatients_env)]
victim_env      <- labels(env)[grep("^V", allpatients_env)]
community_env   <- labels(env)[grep("^LA", allpatients_env)]

Distance-based trees

Calculate a neighbour-joining tree using a TN93 substitution model.

D_env_TN93 <- ape::dist.dna(env, model="TN93", as.matrix=TRUE)
tree_env_NJ_TN93 <- phangorn::NJ(D_env_TN93)

Check if the tree is rooted and if not, plot the unrooted tree.

if (!is.rooted(tree_env_NJ_TN93)) {
    ape::plot.phylo(tree_env_NJ_TN93, type = "unrooted", cex=0.5, lab4ut = "axial")
}

We assume the outgroup is AY156745.1|LA02.EN (one of the community sequences) and root the neighbour-joining tree on it, before plotting the rooted tree.

  tree_env_NJ_TN93_rooted <- root.phylo(tree_env_NJ_TN93,  
                                        outgroup = "AY156745.1|LA02.EN", 
                                        resolve.root = TRUE)
  plot.phylo(tree_env_NJ_TN93_rooted, align.tip.label = TRUE, cex=0.5)

Now create a colour array to colour patient, victim and community sequences differently using the tip.color argument in plot.phylo().

    colours <- rep("black", length(allpatients_env))
    colours[match(patient_env, tree_env_NJ_TN93_rooted$tip.label)] <- 'blue'
    colours[match(victim_env, tree_env_NJ_TN93_rooted$tip.label)] <- 'red'
    
    plot.phylo(tree_env_NJ_TN93_rooted, align.tip.label = TRUE, cex=0.5, 
               tip.color = colours)
    tiplabels(NA, pch=16, col=colours)
    legend("bottomleft", legend=c("Patient", "Victim", "Environment"),
           col=c("blue", "red", "black"), pch=16, cex=0.8)

Build the neighbour-joining tree using a Jukes-Cantor model, plot the tree and then plot the tanglegram connecting the Jukes Cantor NJ tree to the TN93 NJ tree.

# Construct and root Neighbour-joining tree
D_env_JC <- ape::dist.dna(env, model="JC", as.matrix=TRUE)
tree_env_NJ_JC <- phangorn::NJ(D_env_JC)
tree_env_NJ_JC_rooted <- root.phylo(tree_env_NJ_JC,  
                                    outgroup = "AY156745.1|LA02.EN", 
                                    resolve.root = TRUE)

# Plot the tree
plot.phylo(tree_env_NJ_JC_rooted, align.tip.label = TRUE, cex=0.5, 
           tip.color = colours)
legend("bottomleft", legend=c("Patient", "Victim", "Environment"),
           col=c("blue", "red", "black"), pch=16, cex=0.8)

# Plot the tanglegram
cophyloplot(tree_env_NJ_JC_rooted, tree_env_NJ_TN93_rooted, assoc=cbind(tree_env_NJ_JC_rooted$tip.label, tree_env_NJ_JC_rooted$tip.label), show.tip.label = FALSE, space=100, gap =5, length.line=0.2)

Parsimony

Calculate the parsimony score of the NJ tree. To use the function we have to first convert the alignment to an object of type phyDat, which is used by the phangorn package.

  env_phyDat <- as.phyDat(env)  
  parsimony(tree_env_NJ_JC, env_phyDat)
## [1] 1739

Now use the pratchet() function to find the maximum parsimony tree and then use acctran() to adjust the branch lengths, and again plot the tree.

  # Calculate the maximum parsimony tree
  tree_env_MP <- pratchet(env_phyDat)
## Parsimony score of initial tree: 1610 
## Iteration: 10. Best parsimony score so far: 1610Iteration: 20. Best parsimony score so far: 1610Iteration: 30. Best parsimony score so far: 1610Iteration: 40. Best parsimony score so far: 1610Iteration: 50. Best parsimony score so far: 1610Iteration: 60. Best parsimony score so far: 1609Iteration: 70. Best parsimony score so far: 1609Iteration: 80. Best parsimony score so far: 1609Iteration: 90. Best parsimony score so far: 1609Iteration: 100. Best parsimony score so far: 1609
  tree_env_MP <- acctran(tree_env_MP, data = env_phyDat)
  
  # Plot it
  colours <- rep("black", length(tree_env_MP$tip.label))
  colours[match(patient_env, tree_env_MP$tip.label)] <- 'blue'
  colours[match(victim_env, tree_env_MP$tip.label)] <- 'red'
    
  plot.phylo(tree_env_MP, align.tip.label = TRUE, cex=0.7, 
               tip.color = colours)
  tiplabels(NA, pch=16, col=colours)
  legend("bottomleft", legend=c("Patient", "Victim", "Environment"),
       col=c("blue", "red", "black"), pch=16, cex=0.8)

We can also verify that the maximum parsimony tree has a lower parsimony score than the NJ tree.

  parsimony(tree_env_NJ_TN93, env_phyDat)
## [1] 1743
  parsimony(tree_env_MP, env_phyDat)
## [1] 1609
  parsimony(tree_env_NJ_TN93, env_phyDat) < parsimony(tree_env_MP, env_phyDat)
## [1] FALSE

Now we plot the tanglegram comparing the neighbour-joining tree to the maximum parsimony tree.

  # Plot the tanglegram
  cophyloplot(tree_env_MP, tree_env_NJ_TN93_rooted,
              assoc=cbind(tree_env_MP$tip.label, 
                          tree_env_MP$tip.label), 
              show.tip.label = FALSE, space=100, gap =5, length.line=0.1)

Maximum-likelihood

Calculate the maximum-likelihood tree using the GTR model. Since the tree is unrooted (check it!) also root it on the same outgroup.

  tree_env_ML_GTR <- pml_bb(env, model="GTR")
## optimize edge weights:  -10178.12 --> -10121.46 
## optimize rate matrix:  -10121.46 --> -9800.255 
## optimize edge weights:  -9800.255 --> -9799.444 
## optimize topology:  -9799.444 --> -9790.893  NNI moves:  6 
## optimize rate matrix:  -9790.893 --> -9790.853 
## optimize edge weights:  -9790.853 --> -9790.853 
## optimize topology:  -9790.853 --> -9790.853  NNI moves:  0

## optimize edge weights:  -9768.104 --> -9768.104
  tree_env_ML_GTR_rooted <- root.phylo(tree_env_ML_GTR$tree,  
                                       outgroup = "AY156745.1|LA02.EN", 
                                       resolve.root = TRUE)

Now plot the ML tree.

  colours <- rep("black", length(tree_env_ML_GTR_rooted$tip.label))
  colours[match(patient_env, tree_env_ML_GTR_rooted$tip.label)] <- 'blue'
  colours[match(victim_env, tree_env_ML_GTR_rooted$tip.label)] <- 'red'
    
  plot.phylo(tree_env_ML_GTR_rooted, align.tip.label = TRUE, cex=0.7, 
               tip.color = colours)
  tiplabels(NA, pch=16, col=colours)
  legend("bottomleft", legend=c("Patient", "Victim", "Environment"),
       col=c("blue", "red", "black"), pch=16, cex=0.8)

Tanglegram between the NJ and ML trees

  # Plot the tanglegram
  cophyloplot(tree_env_ML_GTR_rooted, tree_env_NJ_TN93_rooted,
              assoc=cbind(tree_env_ML_GTR_rooted$tip.label, 
                          tree_env_ML_GTR_rooted$tip.label), 
              show.tip.label = FALSE, space=100, gap =5, length.line=0.1)

IQ-TREE

Read in the maximum-likelihood and bionj trees produced by IQ-TREE and root them.

  tree_env_ML_IQTREE <- root.phylo(read.tree(params$iqtree_ml), 
                                       outgroup = "AY156745.1|LA02.EN", 
                                       resolve.root = TRUE)
  tree_env_ML_bionj  <- root.phylo(read.tree(params$iqtree_bionj),
                                       outgroup = "AY156745.1|LA02.EN", 
                                       resolve.root = TRUE)

Plot the maximum-likelihood tree and compare to the R ML tree.

  colours <- rep("black", length(tree_env_ML_IQTREE$tip.label))
  colours[match(patient_env, tree_env_ML_IQTREE$tip.label)] <- 'blue'
  colours[match(victim_env, tree_env_ML_IQTREE$tip.label)] <- 'red'
    
  plot.phylo(tree_env_ML_IQTREE, align.tip.label = TRUE, cex=0.7, 
               tip.color = colours)
  tiplabels(NA, pch=16, col=colours)
  legend("bottomleft", legend=c("Patient", "Victim", "Environment"),
       col=c("blue", "red", "black"), pch=16, cex=0.8)

  # Plot the tanglegram
  cophyloplot(tree_env_ML_GTR_rooted, tree_env_ML_IQTREE,
              assoc=cbind(tree_env_ML_GTR_rooted$tip.label, 
                          tree_env_ML_GTR_rooted$tip.label), 
              show.tip.label = FALSE, space=100, gap =5, length.line=0.1)

Plot the bioNJ tree and compare to the R NJ tree.

  colours <- rep("black", length(tree_env_ML_bionj$tip.label))
  colours[match(patient_env, tree_env_ML_bionj$tip.label)] <- 'blue'
  colours[match(victim_env, tree_env_ML_bionj$tip.label)] <- 'red'
    
  plot.phylo(tree_env_ML_bionj, align.tip.label = TRUE, cex=0.7, 
               tip.color = colours)
  tiplabels(NA, pch=16, col=colours)
  legend("bottomleft", legend=c("Patient", "Victim", "Environment"),
       col=c("blue", "red", "black"), pch=16, cex=0.8)


  # Plot the tanglegram
  cophyloplot(tree_env_NJ_TN93_rooted, tree_env_ML_bionj,
              assoc=cbind(tree_env_NJ_TN93_rooted$tip.label, 
                          tree_env_NJ_TN93_rooted$tip.label), 
              show.tip.label = FALSE, space=100, gap =5, length.line=0.1)
